hypothesis: If three knowledge-transfer strategies—pre-training on ChEMBL and ZINC-15, fine-tuning on a curated Covalent Triazine Framework dataset, and reinforcement learning with expert-defined rewards for flatness, symmetry, conjugation, steric hindrance, and pattern matching—are applied to generate Covalent Triazine Frameworks for supercapacitors, then the generated candidates will show higher validity, uniqueness, internal diversity, novelty, and a greater fraction passing expert filters (and closer Fréchet ChemNet Distance) than configurations without this transfer-learning/reward setup, because transfer learning provides domain-specific chemical knowledge and the rewards bias generation toward chemically plausible, structurally aligned CTFs.
